headline: Oil and US gas prices retreat on US-Iran ceasefire
summary: While US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are mixed as investors continue to digest the Trump administration's reported peace deal with Iran, oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) have sunk below $80 per barrel but remain elevated above pre-war prices. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré assess the impact these geopolitical headlines are having on oil and domestic US gas prices (RB=F).

content.title: Oil and US gas prices retreat on US-Iran ceasefire
content.description:
While US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are mixed as investors continue to digest the Trump administration's reported peace deal with Iran, oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) have sunk below $80 per barrel but remain elevated above pre-war prices.

Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré assess the impact these geopolitical headlines are having on oil and domestic US gas prices (RB=F).

headline: It Wasn’t a Strait of Hormuz Relief Rally, After All
summary: Why didn’t anything but tech rally on Monday after the U.S. and Iran announced an agreement that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz?  Peter Boockvar, chief investment officer of One Point BFG Wealth Partners, notes that if the market was reacting to the news and lower oil prices, more of the stocks that are exposed to both would have rallied.  Walmart, Costco, and Target were among the stocks that were down despite the prospect of lower gas prices.

content.title: It Wasn’t a Strait of Hormuz Relief Rally, After All
content.summary: Why didn’t anything but tech rally on Monday after the U.S. and Iran announced an agreement that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz?  Peter Boockvar, chief investment officer of One Point BFG Wealth Partners, notes that if the market was reacting to the news and lower oil prices, more of the stocks that are exposed to both would have rallied.  Walmart, Costco, and Target were among the stocks that were down despite the prospect of lower gas prices.

headline: Playing It Safe at 63 With $850,000 in Cash and Bonds Is Quietly Costing This Retiree About $34,000 a Year
summary: The scenario looks like this: a 63-year-old has built up $850,000 over a working lifetime, watched 2022 and a few scary headlines since, and parked almost all of it in CDs, money market funds, and short Treasuries paying roughly 4%. That throws off about $34,000 a year in interest. It feels prudent. It is also ... Playing It Safe at 63 With $850,000 in Cash and Bonds Is Quietly Costing This Retiree About $34,000 a Year

headline: Walmart (WMT) Faces Class Action Over Massachusetts Hiring Practices
summary: Walmart (NasdaqGS:WMT) is facing a new class action lawsuit in Massachusetts over its hiring practices. The complaint alleges applicants were required to disclose criminal records and complete honesty testing in ways that may conflict with state law. The case could affect thousands of past job applicants and raise questions about Walmart's broader employment practices. Walmart is a major retailer across the United States, with a large workforce and constant hiring needs. That scale means...
